title: TREK plugin registry entry
description: One registry/plugins/<id>.json file. reviewedAt and boundOwner are maintained by CI/publish and may be omitted by submitters. Submissions are strict: no fields beyond $defs/entry. The published dist/index.json additionally carries a CI-computed downloadCount per entry — that shape is described by registry.schema.json, never by a submission.

id.description: Unique slug. Must equal the filename (without .json).

repo.description: GitHub owner/name. Source of truth for the plugin code.

icon.description: A lucide icon name in PascalCase (e.g. "Luggage"), shown on the plugin's tile in TREK's store. Usually the same icon the plugin's trek-plugin.json declares. Omit to get the default (Blocks). CI rejects a name lucide doesn't have.

authorPublicKey.description: Optional base64 minisign (Ed25519) public key. Stable across versions; TREK pins it on first install (TOFU).

trek.description: The TREK versions this version supports, as a semver RANGE (e.g. ">=3.2.0 <4.0.0"). Mirrors the plugin manifest's `trek` field verbatim, and is the ONLY compatibility field a new entry needs — it is what TREK gates installs and activation on. Optional in the schema only so entries published before it existed still validate; scripts/validate-entry.mjs REQUIRES it on the version being published.

minTrekVersion.description: DEPRECATED — superseded by `trek`, which says the same thing and can also express an upper bound. Do not set it on a new entry. It is the range's lower bound, and survives only so that a TREK predating `trek` has something to read; TREK >= 3.4.0 ignores it whenever `trek` is present. If you do set it, CI enforces that it equals the lower bound of `trek` — the two must never disagree.

maxTrekVersion.description: Legacy INCLUSIVE upper bound. Never derived from `trek` (which is exclusive at its top end, so synthesising one would be off by one) — prefer `trek`.

nativeModules.description: Native .node binaries are forbidden in v1.

operatorEgress.description: The plugin needs OPERATOR-supplied egress hosts: its manifest egress[] is not its full network reach, because an admin may add hosts after install. Must mirror the manifest. Defaults to false.

signature.description: Optional base64 minisign signature over the artifact bytes. Requires authorPublicKey on the entry — a cross-level rule the schema cannot express, enforced by scripts/validate-entry.mjs.

requiredAddons.description: Addon ids that must be enabled in TREK for this version to activate. Mirrors the plugin manifest.

pluginDependencies.description: Other plugins this version depends on, each pinned by a semver range. Mirrors the plugin manifest.
